creator: kraileth@elderlinux.org
text:
In December I mailed the maintainer a patch to upgrade the ancient 4.3.9 to 4.4.11 at least but unfortunately never got any reply so far. This Friday 4.4.12 was released with another critical fix, so I wanted to try my luck here on the public bugtracker and found your work from January.

As you wrote, an in-place upgrade is not possible from 4.x to 8.x. I also had a 5.2.4 version that I played with. My plan was to get teleport updated to 4.4.11 (well, .12 now), build a test cluster, upgrade to latest teleport 5.x and propose the next update after one quarterly package roll-over. I'm not sure, how many Teleport users there are on FreeBSD (probably not many because 4.3.x has been pretty much worthless for a long time), but still going the long way of upgrading the port through all the major versions one at a time every 3 months may make sense? Not sure, though.

I will try out your updated port and would love to see a newer version hit the ports tree (I'm mostly after a recent tsh for my FreeBSD workstation). But maybe it might make sense to get it in as "teleport8" or something? While 4.4 is also quite old, it is still supported by upstream - and some people might appreciate the more gentle way of updating instead of rebuilding.
